name: Button
description: An interactive control that triggers a single user action. The primary building block for calls-to-action, form submissions, and destructive confirmations. Supports four semantic variants (primary, secondary, ghost, danger), three sizes, loading state, and icon slots.

intent.purpose: Trigger a discrete user action with clear visual affordance and accessible feedback.
task_context: navigation to a new workflow step

forbids: nested interactive elements (links, other buttons, inputs)

primary.use_when: The action is the main call-to-action on the page or in a form. Use at most once per distinct view or dialog.
primary.avoid_when: There are multiple equally weighted actions — use secondary instead to avoid competing focal points.

secondary.use_when: The action is important but not the primary call-to-action. Good for 'Cancel', 'Back', or alternative paths.
secondary.avoid_when: The action is truly the main next step — use primary. If the action is purely navigational, consider a TextLink.

ghost.use_when: The action should be visually minimal — inline with content, in a toolbar, or as a tertiary option that doesn't compete for attention.
ghost.avoid_when: The action is critical or destructive — use primary or danger. Ghost buttons lack visual weight and can be overlooked.

danger.use_when: The action is destructive or irreversible (delete, remove, revoke access). Requires visual warning.
danger.avoid_when: The action is reversible or non-destructive — use primary or secondary. Overusing danger dilutes its signal.

track: disabled-click (user attempted action on disabled button)
health: primary variant used at most once per view
danger variant always inside a confirmation flow
no more than 3 buttons in a single button group

keyboard.Tab: move focus to next focusable element
keyboard.Shift+Tab: move focus to previous focusable element

accessibility.announces: Button label. When loading: 'Loading, please wait' via aria-busy and sr-only text. When disabled: conveyed via aria-disabled (not HTML disabled, to keep button in tab order).
